2604.26182.title: Lifting Embodied World Models for Planning and Control
2604.26182.paper_summary: World models of embodied agents predict future observations conditioned on an action taken by the agent. For complex embodiments, action spaces are high-dimensional and difficult to specify: for example, precisely controlling a human agent requires specifying the motion of each joint. This makes the world model hard to control and expensive to plan with as search-based methods like CEM scale poorly with action dimensionality. To address this issue, we train a lightweight policy that maps high-level actions to sequences of low-level joint actions. Composing this policy with the frozen world model produces a lifted world model that predicts a sequence of future observations from a single high-level action. We instantiate this framework for a human-like embodiment, defining the high-level action space as a small set of 2D waypoints annotated on the current observation frame, each specifying a near-term goal position for a leaf joint (pelvis, head, hands). Waypoints are low-dimensional, visually interpretable, and easy to specify manually or to search over. We show that the lifted world model substantially outperforms searching directly in low-level joint space ($3.8\times$ lower mean joint error to the goal pose), while remaining more compute-efficient and generalizing to environments unseen by the policy.
2604.26182.paper_summary_zh: 世界模型的具身代理預測基於代理所採取的行動的未來觀察。對於複雜的具身體系，行動空間是高維的且難以具體化：例如，精確控制一個人類代理需要指定每個關節的運動。這使得世界模型難以控制，並且在規劃時成本高昂，因為基於搜索的方法如CEM在行動維度上擴展性差。為了解決這個問題，我們訓練了一個輕量級策略，將高階行動映射到低階關節行動的序列。將此策略與凍結的世界模型組合，產生了一個提升的世界模型，從單一的高階行動預測一系列未來觀察。我們為類人具身體系實現了這個框架，將高階行動空間定義為一小組標註在當前觀察幀上的2D路徑點，每個路徑點指定一個近端目標位置給一個葉子關節（骨盆、頭部、手）。路徑點是低維的，視覺上可解釋，並且容易手動指定或進行搜索。我們顯示提升的世界模型在直接搜索低階關節空間時表現顯著優於（目標姿勢的平均關節誤差低$3.8\times$），同時仍然更具計算效率並能夠泛化到策略未見過的環境中。
2604.26182.author: Alex N. Wang et.al.
2604.26182.authors: Alex N. Wang, Trevor Darrell, Pavel Izmailov, Yutong Bai, Amir Bar
